refsect3.title: Byte Counts And Binary Data
refsect3.para:
Options, such as ipsec-max-bytes, that expect a numeric byte count, also accept decimal fractions, and the following multipliers as a suffix:
KiB
kilobytes (1024 bytes)
MiB
megabytes (1024*1024 bytes)
GiB
gigabytes (1024*1024*1024 bytes)
TiB
terabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)
PiB
petabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)
EiB
exabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)

refsect3.title: Duration
refsect3.para:
Options, such as retransmit-timeout, that expect a numeric duration, also accept decimal fractions, and the following multipliers as a suffix:
us
microseconds (1/1_000_000 seconds)
ms
milliseconds (1/1_000 seconds)
s
seconds
m
minutes (60 seconds)
h
hours (60*60 seconds)
d
days (24*60*60 seconds)
w
weeks (7*24*60*60 seconds)
For instance, the following are each equivalent: 1ms and 1000us; 0.5s and 500ms; 1.5h and 90m.

refsect3.title: Proposals
refsect3.para: The options ike=, esp=, and ah= expect algorithm proposals that follow the following syntax:

Proposals are not required. When omitted, &Libreswan; will provide suitable defaults (see &ipsec-algparse.8;). Similarly, when an algorithm is omitted from a proposal, &Libreswan; will, again, provide suitable defaults.

Specifying the IKE INTEGRITY (hash) algorithms is not recommended; &Libreswan; will automatically generate the prefered INTEGRITY algorithms based on the specified, or default, PRF (KDF) algorithms. Specifying different IKE INTEGRITY and PRF (KDF) is not recommended.
Specifying an additional key-exchange (for instance, ike=aes;addke1=ml_kem_768) in an IKE proposal implicitly enables the IKE_INTERMEDIATE exchange it depends on.

Specifying Key Exchange Methods in either an &ESP; or &AH; proposal is not recommended. When a proposal requires Key Exchange Methods (for instance when PFS is enabled) the proposal will automatically include the Key Exchange Method negotiated during the IKE exchange.
Enabled cryptographic algorithms and default proposals are logged by &pluto; during startup (see &ipsec-pluto.8;).

note.para: Some distributions, such as Fedora and RHEL/CentOS, use a System Wide Crypto Policy to specify the default ike= and esp= proposals. Specifying your own proposals using either ike= or esp= will override these system or software recommended defaults (but can be necessary at times).

refsect3.para: History: the ";TYPE=ALGORITHMS" syntax was added in &Libreswan; 5.4; the "ENCRYPTION-INTEGRITY-PRF" syntax for &IKE; proposals was deprecated in &Libreswan; 5.4; the "ENCRYPTION-PRF-INTEGRITY" syntax for &IKE; proposals was added in &Libreswan; 5.4.
Limitations: &Libreswan; 5.4 does not support the IKE_FOLLOWUP_KE exchange.
Note: The abbreviation "KEM" can refer to either Key Exchange Method or Key Encapsulation Method.
